# HamGlobe: Put your logs on the map

HamGlobe is a tool that allows you to visualize your logs on a map. It is designed to work with Amateur Radio logs, but can be used with any log that contains path (point to point) information.

## Features

- Visualize your logs on a map
- Embed map in your website
- Export map as an image
- Support for ADIF logs
- QRZ integration for station information
- Customizable map styles
- Filter logs by date, band, mode, etc.
- Optional self-hosted service for use on your own domain

## Installation

Hamglobe is available as a web application and can be accessed at [https://hamglobe.com](https://hamglobe.com).

## Usage

Upload your log file in ADIF format, and HamGlobe will automatically parse the data and display it on the map. You can customize the map style, filter logs by various criteria, and will be available for embedding or sharing through a unique URL or API.

## License & Terms of Use

HamGlobe is a source-visible but paid service. It is not certified "open source" or "FOSS". You may use the free version with limited features, or subscribe to the premium version for full access. By using HamGlobe, you agree to our [Terms of Service](TOS.md) and [Privacy Policy](PRIVACY.md). Please review these documents before using the service and discontinue usage if you do not agree with the terms.

If you would like to contribute to the development of HamGlobe, please read [Contributing.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) for guidelines on how to get involved. We welcome contributions from the community to help improve the tool and add new features, but please note that all contributions will be subject to review and may not be accepted if they do not align with our goals or standards, and any contributions that are merged into the project will be subject to the same licensing terms as the rest of the codebase. Compensation for contributions will be determined on a case-by-case basis and is not guaranteed.
